Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Complex [The Juicy Mix campaign, a group with basic skill levels, has launched a sophisticated cyber attack targeting healthcare organizations. They employ a multi-stage approach involving reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact. Their aim is to encrypt data and disrupt operations while maintaining stealth and persistence within the network.]
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Network Traffic, User Account, Target Artifact (Windows currentversion), Application Log, Logon Session, Target Artifact (Channel)
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism), Response Metadata (Mechanism), Bash Script, Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder, Application Log Content (Mechanism), User Account Authentication (Mechanism), System Network Configuration Discovery, Remote Desktop Protocol, Logon Session Creation (Mechanism), Exfiltration Over C2 Channel, Data Encrypted for Impact

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 309 Name: Network Topology Mapping
stage.description: The attackers initiate network scans to identify potential vulnerabilities within the healthcare organization's network infrastructure.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 522 Name: Malicious Hardware Component Replacement
stage.description: The attackers establish fake domains and other resources to support their phishing campaigns and subsequent attacks.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2018-0172 (Suggested): Denial-of-Service Vulnerability in Cisco IOS Software
stage.description: Employees are targeted with phishing emails containing malicious links or attachments leading to initial access.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2023-20887 (Suggested): VMware Aria Operations for Networks Command Injection Vulnerability
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 88 Name: OS Command Injection
stage.description: Malware is executed via command-line interfaces post-initial access.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.technique_name: T1060: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 270 Name: Modification of Registry Run Keys
stage.description: Malware is added to the startup folder to maintain persistent access upon reboot.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 121 Name: Exploit Non-Production Interfaces
stage.description: Unpatched software vulnerabilities are exploited to escalate privileges within the compromised environment.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 578 Name: Disable Security Software
stage.description: Defensive tools such as antivirus software are disabled or modified to avoid detection.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 49 Name: Password Brute Forcing
stage.description: Repeated attempts are made to guess login credentials to gain unauthorized access.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 309 Name: Network Topology Mapping
stage.description: The attackers gather information about the network configuration to understand the layout and potential pathways for lateral movement.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 220 Name: Client-Server Protocol Manipulation
stage.description: Attackers move laterally across the network using RDP to access other systems.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 277 Name: Data Interchange Protocol Manipulation
stage.description: Sensitive data is collected from various internal repositories.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 24 Name: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: Stolen data is transferred to attacker-controlled servers over a command and control channel.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 609 Name: Cellular Traffic Intercept
stage.description: Organizational data is encrypted to disrupt operations and potentially extort payment from the victim.

Scenario.summary: The Juicy Mix campaign employs a complex multi-stage attack strategy against healthcare organizations, focusing on gaining initial access through phishing, establishing persistence, escalating privileges, evading defenses, accessing credentials, discovering the network, moving laterally, collecting sensitive data, exfiltrating it, and finally impacting the organization by encrypting critical data.
